verdicts.note: Evidence confirms OAuth2 (3-legged flow) and API key (personal access tokens) as Bearer tokens are supported authentication methods.

Evidence confirms API access requires paid Business/Enterprise/Advanced Work Management plans, with self-serve token generation via Personal Settings or OAuth2 implementation once on a qualifying plan.
Evidence confirms REST API at base URL https://api.smartsheet.com/2.0 with OpenAPI spec, covering sheets, rows, workspaces, users, alternate emails, etc., and mentions multiple SDKs.

The URL https://mcp.smartsheet.com resolves but the evidence page content is empty/blank. No concrete evidence of a dedicated MCP server implementation is visible. The evidence does not demonstrate this is an actual MCP server - it could be a placeholder domain. Without verifiable MCP server code, documentation, or implementation details, this should be marked as 'unknown' with no URL.
Evidence confirms 'buildable-with-caveats' is appropriate - API access requires a paid Business/Enterprise/Advanced Work Management plan with no free tier, which is a clear caveat to building.
Evidence confirms API access is restricted to Business and Enterprise plans with no free tier for API access, matching the stated main blocker.
All evidence URLs are accessible and support their stated claims, except mcp.smartsheet.com which shows no visible content to confirm an MCP server exists.

revised.name: Smartsheet
revised.one_liner: Smartsheet offers a comprehensive REST API with OAuth2 or personal access tokens authentication, but API access requires a paid Business/Enterprise/Advanced Work Management plan.

auth_methods.notes: 3-legged OAuth 2.0 flow for third-party apps requiring user consent; Bearer token used in Authorization header.
Personal access token generated via Smartsheet UI (Personal Settings) for machine-to-machine integrations; used as Bearer token.

revised.self_serve_notes: No free tier for API access; requires Business, Enterprise, or Advanced Work Management plan. Once on a qualifying plan, developers can self-serve generate a personal access token via Personal Settings or implement OAuth2 without needing sales approval.

api_surface.notes: Full REST API (base https://api.smartsheet.com/2.0) with OpenAPI spec covering sheets, rows, workspaces, users, alternate emails, etc. SDKs available in multiple languages (Java, C#, PHP, Go, Ruby, R, Python, Node.js/JS). Multi-region endpoints available (US/EU/AU).

revised.main_blocker: API access is gated behind a paid Business/Enterprise/Advanced Work Management plan; no free-tier self-serve credentials.

evidence.supports: OAuth2 and API access token (Bearer) authentication methods
Personal access token generation and API restricted to Business/Enterprise plans
No free tier includes API access; requires paid plan
URL exists but no visible content to confirm MCP server implementation
